message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose AACE alignment fns + bdate_range (fd90.269 / br-aywnz)

The README's "Index Alignment: The AACE Pipeline" section (lines
280-302) documents alignment as a first-class user-visible concept,
but the actual functions were unreachable from frankenpandas.

Top-level fp_index re-export (12 items):
- align, align_inner, align_left, align_union (4 alignment modes;
  AlignMode enum was already exposed)
- validate_alignment_plan
- leapfrog_intersection, leapfrog_union, multi_way_align
  (AG-05 multi-way alignment)
- bdate_range (pandas pd.bdate_range business-day range)
- format_datetime_ns
- infer_freq_from_nanos, infer_freq_from_timestamps
  (timestamp/nanos variants of infer_freq)

Prelude (1):
- bdate_range alongside date_range / timedelta_range

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-aywnz","title":"fd90.269: Expose AACE Pipeline alignment fns + bdate_range","description":"fd90.269: Expose AACE Pipeline alignment functions + bdate_range / format_datetime_ns.

The README's "Index Alignment: The AACE Pipeline" section (lines 280-302) documents alignment as a first-class user-visible concept. Three alignment functions are missing from frankenpandas:
- align, align_inner, align_left, align_union (4 modes — AlignMode is already exposed)
- validate_alignment_plan
- leapfrog_intersection, leapfrog_union, multi_way_align (AG-05 multi-way alignment)
- bdate_range (business-day date range — pandas pd.bdate_range equivalent)
- format_datetime_ns
- infer_freq_from_nanos, infer_freq_from_timestamps (timestamp/nanos variants of infer_freq)

Scope:
- Add 12 functions to top-level fp_index re-export
- Add bdate_range to prelude (matches user-facing date_range / timedelta_range pattern from fd90.261)

 {"id":"frankenpandas-b6o","title":"Fix timedelta fmt and clippy blockers","description":"Workspace validation after FP-P2D-131 exposed lingering fp-types timedelta rustfmt drift plus clippy blockers for identity multiplication and inconsistent digit grouping. Clean these narrow issues so workspace clippy remains green without touching broader unreserved formatting drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:16:43.983939665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:19:55.107860309Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:19:55.105957839Z","close_reason":"Fixed timedelta fmt and clippy digit grouping","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","formatting","hygiene"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-b7yru","title":"fd90.112: README df.clip(f64, f64) wants Option<f64> + df.replace(&S, &S) wants &[(S, S)] slice","description":"fd90.74-111 README audit. Two arity/type mismatches in element-wise op examples:

1. Line 1049: 'let clipped = df.clip(0.0, 100.0)?;' but
   crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:3690 declares
   pub fn clip(&self, lower: Option<f64>, upper: Option<f64>) -> Result<Self, FrameError>
   The args are Option<f64>, not f64. A user copy-pasting hits:
   error[E0308]: expected enum 'Option', found floating-point number

2. Line 1060: 'let cleaned = df.replace(&Scalar::Int64(-999), &Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN))?;'
   but crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:3571 declares
   pub fn replace(&self, replacements: &[(Scalar, Scalar)]) -> Result<Self, FrameError>
   replace takes a slice of (from, to) pairs, NOT two separate Scalar args. A user
   copy-pasting hits:
   error[E0061]: this method takes 1 argument but 2 arguments were supplied

Fix: Wrap the clip args in Some(...) and convert the replace example to a slice of pairs. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:44:50.805093041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:46:11.542730041Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:46:11.540037282Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
